messages.content: How can we create a triangle shape layer pointing left and place it in the center of document using Photoshop scripting?
What is the width and height ratio of equal 60 degree triangle?

An equilateral triangle (where all sides and angles are the same) has angles of 60 degrees each. The width-to-height ratio of an equilateral triangle can be determined using basic trigonometry.

If we consider the height of the triangle (the altitude) to be 'h' and the length of a side to be 'a', we can split the triangle in half along the altitude to create two 30-60-90 right triangles. The altitude 'h' will be opposite the 60-degree angle, the half-length of a side (a/2) will be opposite the 30-degree angle, and the full length of the side 'a' is the hypotenuse of the right triangle.

For a 30-60-90 triangle, the ratios of the sides are:
- The side opposite the 30-degree angle (a/2) is 1/2 the hypotenuse (a).
- The side opposite the 60-degree angle (h) is √3/2 times the hypotenuse (a).

So the height (altitude) 'h' of an equilateral triangle is given by:
h = (√3/2) * a

Now if we want the width-to-height ratio:
The width of the equilateral triangle is simply the length of a side 'a'.
So the width-to-height ratio is:
ratio = a / h
ratio = a / ((√3/2) * a)
ratio = 2 / √3
ratio = 2√3 / 3 (by rationalizing the denominator)

Thus, the width-to-height ratio of an equilateral 60-degree triangle is 2√3:3, which is approximately 1.1547:1. This means the width is slightly larger than the height by a factor of about 1.1547.
How can we translate into Photoshop Scripting? Imagine our height is 100 pixel.
Error on this line:     fillLayer.applyColor(colorDesc);
Error on this line:     executeAction(charIDToTypeID('Mk  '), desc, DialogModes.NO);
It gives an error :     executeAction(cTID('Mk  '), desc1, DialogModes.NO);
Now it gives an error on this line:   executeAction(cTID("Mk  "), descTrianglePath, DialogModes.NO);

It still gives an error for this line, please try another solution :   executeAction(cTID('Mk  '), desc, DialogModes.NO);
Also please create a debug so we can understand the problem
